title: ‘No Kings’ protest movement announces March rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The 'No Kings' protest rally is scheduled on Boston Common on March 28 from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m.
Organized by the ACLU of Massachusetts and others, the movement opposes authoritarianism and promotes democratic reforms.
The March rally aims to be the largest, following last October's event that drew over 100,000 demonstrators.

title: Now’s the time to book that Cape Cod getaway
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Travelers should book Cape Cod summer vacations early, as rentals are already 65% occupied for July and August.
Cape Cod was recently named a top destination for Memorial Day weekend getaways by Conde Nast Traveler.
Chatham Bars Inn on Cape Cod received a five-star rating from the Forbes Travel Guide in 2026.

title: Charlie McAvoy’s reward as Bruins’ OT hero? Another trip to the dentist’s chair.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the overtime winner in Bruins' 2-1 victory over the Los Angeles Kings on Tuesday.
Despite being the game's hero, McAvoy required immediate dental work due to persistent on-ice injuries.
The 28-year-old defenseman continues to play through physical challenges, aiding Bruins' playoff push.

title: After her son was struck by a car, she’s running the Boston Marathon for Newton firefighters
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Maureen Murphy is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Newton Firefighters Children's Fund.
Murphy's son, Teddy, was struck by a car, and first aid was provided by Newton Fire Department.
The fundraiser supports children of fallen firefighters across the Commonwealth, expressing gratitude to first responders.

title: Victor Wembanyama shines, Jaylen Brown is ejected as Celtics drop chaotic loss to Spurs: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Victor Wembanyama scored 39 points, including 8-for-15 from three-point range, as Spurs beat Celtics 125-116.
Jaylen Brown was ejected after two technical fouls following a missed call, impacting Celtics' offensive efforts.
Derrick White led Celtics with 34 points, while Jayson Tatum scored 24 points despite efficiency struggles.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (March 11)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family Colonial in Andover sold for $1,778,500 with 10 rooms, 5 bedrooms, and 4 baths on a 39,426 sq ft lot.
A two-family conventional house in Allston was sold for $1,202,000, featuring 13 rooms, 9 bedrooms, and 2 baths.
A one-family row-middle home in Boston, built in 1900, was sold for $22,000,000, encompassing 18 rooms and 9 baths.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the game-winning goal in overtime to give the Bruins a 2-1 victory over the Kings.
Despite a bloodied mouth and missing teeth, McAvoy played through pain to help Bruins secure their 13th straight home win.
Coach Marco Sturm praised McAvoy's resilience, highlighting his potential for leadership in the Bruins' alternate captain setup.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected from the Celtics-Spurs game after receiving two technical fouls in the second quarter.
Brown criticized referee Tyler Ford on social media following his ejection, expressing frustration with the officiating.
This marks Brown's second career ejection; his absence contributed to the Celtics' 125-116 loss to the Spurs.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, sentenced to life with parole possibility after 17 years for murdering Margarita Morehead in Chelsea.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to multiple charges including second-degree murder and violating a protection order in February 2026.
Morehead was fatally stabbed on May 8, 2023, after Alvarez punched her during an argument at her home.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: MBTA Transit Police are searching for a suspect involved in an assault at Prudential station on February 27 at 8 p.m.
The suspect allegedly spat on and struck a Green Line rider multiple times with a closed fist.
Photos show the suspect in black clothing with short hair, a beard, and tattoos on neck and hands.
